version_history.changes: Initial Release of MIDI-CI Profile implementation of MPE

M2-100-U MIDI 2.0 Specification Overview, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
M2-101-UM MIDI Capability Inquiry (MIDI-CI), Version 1.2, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
M2-102-U Common Rules for MIDI-CI Profiles, Version 1.1, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
M2-104-UM Universal MIDI Packet (UMP) Format and MIDI 2.0 Protocol, Version 1.1, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
MIDI-CI Property Exchange Controller Resources, Version 0.61.0, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
MIDI 2.0 Bit Scaling and Resolution, Version 1.0.2, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
M1-101-UM MIDI Polyphonic Expression, Version 1.1, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
MIDI Polyphonic Expression (RP53), Version 1.0, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/
M1-117-UM Property Exchange Controller Resources, Version 1.0, Association of Musical Electronics Industry, http://www.amei.or.jp/, and The MIDI Association, https://www.midi.org/

definitions.description: A unit of measure for musical intervals, corresponding to one-twelfth of an octave measured logarithmically. This term is preferred over “semitone” which may refer to various intervals.
Any note for which a Note On message has been delivered, but a Note Off message has not.
Association of Musical Electronics Industry. Authority for MIDI Specifications in Japan.

An entity, whether hardware or software, which can send and/or receive MIDI messages and has one or more functional subsystems which generate, consume, and/or route MIDI messages. A Device has one or more MIDI inputs, outputs, or bidirectional connections for sending and/or receiving MIDI messages connected to its functional subsystems.
See 100-Cent-Unit
See MIDI Association.
A MIDI Channel reserved for conveying messages that apply to the entire Zone.
Any MIDI Channel within a Zone that is not a Manager Channel.
Complete MIDI 1.0 Detailed Specification, Document Version 96.1, Third Edition [MA01]
The public facing name that the MIDI Manufacturers Association uses as its DBA.
MIDI Capability Inquiry, a specification published by The MIDI Association and AMEI.

The specification that defines how MIDI Devices communicate multidimensional control data. This document is the most current specification, and the original specification was M1-101-UM MIDI Polyphonic Expression, Version 1.1 [MA08]
A hardware or software MIDI connection used by a Device to transmit and/or receive MIDI messages to and/or from another Device.
See MIDI Manufacturers Association.
A California nonprofit 501(c)6 trade organization, and the legal entity name of the MIDI Association.

Each Member Channel will only play one note at a time. Starting a note in such a Channel, when one is already playing, shall stop the older note, possibly invoking a legato transition between the old and the new notes.
For Polyphonic Channel Response, Program Change is applied only to the Manager Channel and all member Channels are set to the same program.
See MIDI Polyphonic Expression.
Shorthand for MIDI-CI Profile for MIDI Polyphonic Expression
MPE defines three or more dimensions of expression. It’s left to the implementor of an MPE controller to determine what gestures are mapped to the three MPE expression messages.
A Member Channel with at least one Active Note.
Each Member Channel may play more than one note at a time. Starting a note in such a Channel, when one is already playing, shall start a new note.

A MIDI Device which has a MIDI Transport connected to its MIDI In. A MIDI Device is not required to recognize or act upon any specific MIDI messages that it receives in order to be defined as a Receiver.

A note for which a Note Off message has been processed. A Released Note may continue to sound for considerable time, most often owing to the length of a release envelope or an interaction with the sustain or sostenuto pedal.
A MIDI Device which transmits MIDI messages to a MIDI Transport which is connected to its MIDI Out or to its MIDI Thru port.
Any Active or Released Note that is still making sound.
In addition to Pitch Bend and Channel Pressure, MPE controllers may provide a third dimension of continuous control using Control Change #74 (or RC Bank 0x20, index 0x21) see Section 4.5
A Channel for which all Active Notes have received Note Off messages.
Contiguous MIDI Channels comprising a Manager Channel and one or more Member Channels

relating_to_conformance.relation: Mandatory A conformant implementation conforms to all ‘shall’ statements.
Recommended but not mandatory An implementation that does not conform to some or all ‘should’ statements is still conformant, providing all ‘shall’ statements are conformed to.
Optional An implementation that does not conform to some or all ‘may’ statements is still conformant, providing that all ‘shall’ statements are conformed to.

not_relating_to_conformance.relation: Describes an action to be taken that, while not required (or at least not directly required) by this specification, is unavoidable. Not used for statements of conformance requirement (see ‘shall’ above).

Describes a condition that as a question of fact is necessarily going to be true, or an action that as a question of fact is necessarily going to occur, but not as a requirement (or at least not as a direct requirement) of this specification. Not used for statements of conformance requirements (see ‘shall’ above).
Describes a condition or action that a system element is capable of possessing or taking. Not used for statements of conformance permission (see ‘may’ above).
Describes a condition or action that a system element is capable of electing to possess or take. Not used for statements of conformance permission (see ‘may’ above).

channel_response_notification.parameter: byte Device ID: Source or Destination (depending on type of message):
Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Profile Specific Data
0x00 0x00 0x00 Length of Following Profile Specific Data (LSB first)

profile_details_inquiry.parameter: Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Profile Details Inquiry
Inquiry Target: Profile Optional Features Supported

reply_to_profile_details.parameter: Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Reply to Profile Details Inquiry
Inquiry Target: Profile Optional Features Supported
bytes Inquiry Target Data Length (dl) (LSB first)
bytes Inquiry Target Data – Features Supported

fields.description: Supports Channel Response Type Notification See Section 3.6

mpe_expression_controllers.alternate_bipolar_controller: RPN MSB/RC Bank 0x20, RPN LSB/RC Index 0x20
RPN MSB/RC Bank 0x20, RPN LSB/RC Index 0x21

note_on_setup_example.effect: Pitch Bend is sent Quartertone bend upwards, assuming sensitivity has been set to 48 HCUs. This is to ensure that the Channel already has a Pitch Bend value before the Note On message.
Control Change #74 or Bipolar Controller (0x20, 0x21) with an initial value for timbre. This is to ensure that the Channel already has a Third Dimension of Control value before the Note On message.
Set to zero Template Instructions:a)i)(1)(a)(i)Appendix D
Note = Middle C with a velocity

step1.parameter: byte Device ID: Source or Destination (depending on type of message):
To/from MIDI Channels 1-16 (set to desired Manager Channel)
Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Inquiry: Profile Details Inquiry Message
Inquiry Target = Number of MIDI Channels

step2.parameter: byte Device ID: Source or Destination (depending on type of message):
To/from MIDI Channels 1-16 (set to requested Manager Channel)
Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Inquiry: Reply to Profile Details Message
Inquiry Target = Number of MIDI Channels
bytes The number of Channels currently in use by this Profile.
= Total Number of Channels, including Manager and Member Channels. (LSB First). If
Profile is not currently enabled, set to 0x00 0x00.
bytes Maximum Number of Channels (available for use by this Profile).
= Total number of Channels, including Manager and Member Channels. (LSB first)

step3.parameter: To/from MIDI Channels 1-16 (set to desired Manager Channel)
Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Set Profile On
bytes Profile ID of Profile to be Set to On (to be enabled) (0x7E 0x31 0x00 0x01 0x01)
following fields (except F7 End) were added in MIDI-CI Message Version 2
bytes Number of Channels Requested (LSB First) to assign to this Profile when it is enabled

step4.parameter: To/from MIDI Channels 1-16 (set to enabled Manager Channel)
Universal System Exclusive Sub-ID#2: Inquiry: Profile Enabled
following fields (except F7 End) were added in MIDI-CI Message Version 2
bytes Number of Channels enabled on this Profile. (Manager + Member Channels, LSB first)

midi_messages_table: O M P P See Section 3.5
